stemHtml: Which of the following describes a correct tilt setting for an airborne weather radar?

options.a: A high tilt setting is helpful to detect a thunderstorm during a climb.
options.b: During any phase of flight, the tilt of the weather radar will always be adjusted to the optimum tilt.
options.c: During a climb, the tilt needs to be decreased to scan for hazardous clouds.
options.d: A low tilt setting should be used during descent if thunderstorms are expected.
